No Poverty.reason: The course provides 'appropriate new technology' (programming skills) which can lead to economic opportunities and improved livelihoods, indirectly contributing to poverty reduction (Target 1.4).
evidence: 運用程式設計的技術解決實務的問題

Zero Hunger.reason: No direct or indirect evidence in the course syllabus links to food security, nutrition, or sustainable agriculture.

Good Health and Well-being.reason: No direct or indirect evidence in the course syllabus links to health outcomes, disease prevention, or healthcare services.

Quality Education.reason: As a university course, it provides tertiary education (Target 4.3) and technical skills (Python, data analysis) for employment (Target 4.4), fostering '自主與終身學習的態度' (lifelong learning).

Gender Equality.reason: The course enhances 'information and communications technology' skills (Target 5.b), which can indirectly contribute to women's empowerment, though not explicitly stated.

Clean Water and Sanitation.reason: No direct or indirect evidence in the course syllabus links to water quality, sanitation, or hygiene.

Affordable and Clean Energy.reason: No direct or indirect evidence in the course syllabus links to energy access, renewable energy, or energy efficiency.

Decent Work and Economic Growth.reason: The course teaches high-value technical skills (Python, Pandas, scikit-learn) that support 'technological upgrading and innovation' (Target 8.2) and 'decent job creation' (Target 8.3).

Industry, Innovation and Infrastructure.reason: The course is core to enhancing 'scientific research' and 'technological capabilities' (Target 9.5) through programming, data analysis, and machine learning, fostering innovation.

Reduced Inequalities.reason: Providing valuable technical skills through programming can indirectly promote economic inclusion (Target 10.2), though the course does not explicitly address inequality.

Sustainable Cities and Communities.reason: Given the 'Land Economics' context, data analysis (Pandas) and image processing (OpenCV) skills are applicable to sustainable human settlement planning and management (Target 11.3).

Responsible Consumption and Production.reason: No direct or indirect evidence in the course syllabus links to sustainable consumption, production patterns, or waste management.

Climate Action.reason: While programming can be used for climate-related analysis, the syllabus does not specify any direct application or focus on climate change measures.

Life Below Water.reason: No direct or indirect evidence in the course syllabus links to marine ecosystems, pollution, or sustainable fisheries.

Life on Land.reason: In the 'Land Economics' context, image processing (OpenCV) and data analysis (Pandas) skills are applicable to integrating ecosystem values into planning and management (Target 15.9).

Peace, Justice and Strong Institutions.reason: No direct or indirect evidence in the course syllabus links to violence reduction, justice, or institutional development.

Partnerships for the Goals.reason: The course builds capacity in 'information and communications technology' and data analysis (Targets 17.8, 17.18), which are enabling technologies for achieving all SDGs.
